title: Standardize item-level context and provenance for misclassification classification
description:
## Summary
Standardize the evaluation item context and provenance needed to classify a misclassification correctly.

## Deliverables
- Define the canonical per-item context passed into classification, including transcript, prediction, label, score explanation, score configuration, score guidelines, scorecard guidance, feedback comments, and label provenance.
- Ensure the payload can distinguish when feedback-derived context is present versus absent.
- Define what metadata must be persisted so item-level classifications remain auditable after the run completes.

## Notes
This task is about the data contract for classification, not the classifier logic itself.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Kickoff implementation: standardizing per-item misclassification context/provenance contract for evaluation RCA. Immediate focus is one canonical payload shape with explicit source availability markers so downstream classifier/persistence/UI tasks consume the same schema.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented canonical item-level misclassification context/provenance contract and wired it into RCA exemplar payloads (standard and small-set flows). Added locked label-provenance enum, explicit source-availability flags, and persisted-audit metadata requirements in rca_analysis contract. Each exemplar now carries misclassification_item_context with identifiers, prediction context, label provenance, feedback context excerpts, score context excerpts, transcript/metadata availability, and audit metadata. Validation: pytest -q plexus/rca_analysis_test.py plexus/Evaluation_feedback_test.py (21 passed).
